author: @jerryhoward6007
text: Robert, Off subject, a 2x questions/ideas! Why couldn't you make an EV with 2 batteries, running off one while charging the other? Maybe turn all 4 wheels into alternators? & Could ships draw enough power from sea water to be sufficient for anything? Maybe they already do & I just don't know it. Thnx!

replies.author: @David_Mash
replies.text:
The motor in an EV takes energy from the wheels to make itself an "alternator " of sorts. The battery issue is weight, you are proposing doubling the weight but only using half the output at any given time. 
Also an EV does charge the same battery whenever you are not using energy. There is a company proposing to absorb suspension energy and convert it into electricity. You might enjoy that rabbit hole.

replies.author: @AutoNomades
replies.text: Yes, i was wondering maybe a boat could recharge energy when anchored with the moovements up and down while navigators have a rest, then us it for the port motor phase. Then, outside the port, using the sail.. ; )

replies.author: @johnmcfadden9336
replies.text:
This wouldn’t work unfortunately but an idea I read about back in the 80s you might like was to have a tank of compressed air that was used to run a turbine to generate and recharge as you drove, the cables from the batteries to the motor were tubes and utilised the heat from the resistance and the friction from the motor increased the power in the compressed air 
Also someone on YouTube placed a wind generator on the front of his car and managed to get improved mileage which was considered improbable but his trials seemed to be well conducted 
Maybe with the right design this could extend mileage  a bit but I doubt it would be viable

replies.author: @AutoNomades
replies.text: @johnmcfadden9336 Yeah, "Maybe"... What argument do you have to say that "it wouldn't work" ? Maybe if he would have put a larger wind -break- generator it would have work better !!! ^^..

replies.author: @David_Mash
replies.text: @johnmcfadden9336 well you're onto something there. You could put a wind turbine on a vehicle if you could block it while consuming energy. I would like to see more about this compressed air setup. If using a refrigerant, this could be quite interesting and you could use compressing as a brake feature
